title: Cheilanthes pallens
intro: Cheilanthes pallens, also known as the pale lip fern, is a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been associated with various health benefits, including wound healing, the reduction of fever, and the alleviation of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Cheilanthes pallens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cheilanthes pallens is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Cheilanthes pallens is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Cheilanthes, which consists of various species of ferns commonly known as the lip ferns or brittle ferns. The species Cheilanthes pallens is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a non-vascular plant, which means it does not have xylem or phloem tissues. This classification places it within the broader kingdom Plantae and phylum Pteridophyta.
